chunk_summary: This section discusses accurate blood pressure measurement techniques and percentile-based classification for pediatric hypertension in metabolic risk clinics, emphasizing the importance of standardized methods and age-specific thresholds.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of accurate blood pressure measurement in children with obesity-related metabolic risk.
Learn the standardized technique for clinic-based blood pressure measurement in pediatric patients.
Identify age-specific and percentile-based thresholds for classifying pediatric hypertension.
Recognize the limitations of oscillometric devices and the need for auscultatory confirmation.
key_facts: Obesity-related cardiometabolic risk may first present as elevated blood pressure.
For ages 1 to <13 years, normal BP is <90th percentile; elevated BP is ≥90th to <95th percentile; stage 1 hypertension is ≥95th to <95th+12 mmHg; stage 2 hypertension is ≥95th+12 mmHg.
For adolescents ≥13 years, adult-aligned thresholds are used: normal <120/<80 mmHg; elevated 120/<80 to 129/<80 mmHg; stage 1 hypertension 130/80 to 139/89 mmHg; stage 2 hypertension ≥140/90 mmHg.
Oscillometric devices can overestimate BP compared to auscultation; confirm elevated readings with auscultation.
Use a cuff with bladder length encircling 80–100% of arm circumference and width at least 40% of mid-arm circumference.
reference_quote: Accurate BP measurement is foundational because obesity-related cardiometabolic risk may first present as elevated BP, and mismeasurement is a leading cause of over- and under-diagnosis.
document_key_topics: Limitations of oscillometric devices in BP measurement
